title: When Voters Worry About ‘Affordability,’ Many Point to Health Care
summary: Democrats have had limited political success running on the Affordable Care Act, even with its relative popularity. Now President Trump’s health care cuts may have given the issue new resonance.

title: In California, a Republican Sheriff Seizes Ballots, Prompting Concerns
summary: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a candidate for governor, recently took possession of more than 650,000 ballots as part of a fraud probe. Election officials say his investigation is baseless.

title: How Obama’s Strategist Discouraged Biden From Running in 2016
summary: In a new set of oral histories, David Plouffe, President Barack Obama’s political adviser, described how he urged Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. not to challenge Hillary Clinton for the nomination.

title: Trump Delays Energy Strikes, but Iran’s Infrastructure is Already Battered
summary: U.S. and Israeli attacks on power plants and other civilian infrastructure risk escalating the conflict across the region, and angering Iranians who oppose the government.

title: Stocks Bounce as Trump Backs Off Iran Infrastructure Threat
summary: Oil prices tumbled and yields on government bonds pulled back from highs. But the response faded as the day went on.

title: Deadly Plane Crash at LaGuardia Airport: What We Know
summary: Two pilots were killed and dozens of others were injured when a regional jet collided with a fire truck on the runway.

title: Trump Administration to Pay $1 Billion to Energy Giant to Cancel Wind Farms
summary: In exchange, the French company TotalEnergies would invest in oil and natural gas projects in Texas and elsewhere.

title: Trump Is Digging Up Washington. Can Lawsuits Stop the Bulldozers?
summary: As the president develops plans to fundamentally alter the White House, the Kennedy Center and other sites, federal lawsuits are beginning to catch up.

title: New Top Prosecutor Named in Embattled U.S. Attorney’s Office
summary: Federal judges appointed Robert Frazer to run New Jersey’s U.S. attorney’s office, which has been in disarray over the past year because of uncertainty about who was in charge.

title: A Professional’s Guide to Spring Cleaning Your Life
summary: Tackle clutter, let go of sentimental objects and create a home that reflects who you are now.

title: Takeaways From The Times’s Inside Look at the C.D.C.
summary: Many current and former employees say the actions of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. are undermining the agency’s role in safeguarding public health.

title: Born Abroad and Fearful of ICE, Adoptees Try to Prove They Belong
summary: Up to 200,000 people adopted as children from abroad are vulnerable to deportation by an administration searching for problems with their citizenship.

title: Immigrant Detentions and Deportations Leave Pets Behind
summary: As immigrant detentions and self-deportations soar, animal welfare groups in cities like New Orleans scramble to feed, foster and re-home the pets left behind.

title: How Epstein Helped Solve a Billionaire’s Problems With Women
summary: The Wall Street titan Leon Black paid Jeffrey Epstein $170 million for what he said was tax and estate work. But his services went beyond that.

title: What’s It Like to Be Back in Print After 20 Years? A Bit Odd.
summary: Nancy Lemann published her first novel at 28. Then came “the doom.” Now she’s back in the spotlight, and not exactly comfortable with it.

title: Review: On ‘Arirang,’ BTS Is Too Big to Fail. But Not Too Big to Succeed.
summary: The K-pop superstars’ new album, “Arirang,” comes with huge commercial expectations. The group made room for artistic experimentation, too.

title: Why the BTS Comeback Concert Was a ‘Disaster’ for Some Businesses
summary: The turnout for the K-pop titans’ show was much lower than projected by officials, hitting the bottom line of some restaurants. Shares in the group’s management company also fell.

title: We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
summary: Prediction markets teach us to look at the future as gamblers, rather than as citizens.

title: Chuck Schumer: What the SAVE Act Would Really Do
summary: Republicans want to pretend the SAVE Act is a voter ID bill.

title: Death by Paper
summary: We examine a new innovation in drug smuggling.

title: N.Y.U. Professors Go on Strike, Seeking Better Pay and Job Protections
summary: About 950 full-time faculty members who are not on the tenure track walked out. The university told students that classes would continue.

title: Diabetes, Overlooked and Unchecked, Poses New Risks in Africa
summary: As deaths from diabetes start to rival those from infectious threats like malaria, a new form of the condition linked to malnutrition is surfacing in patients who can afford neither screening nor care.

title: A Choice of Deadly Drones Is Only a Few Clicks Away for Ukrainian Troops
summary: Ukraine has created online marketplaces to let units select their own drones, a break from generations of standardized and centralized weapons procurement.
